paper_title: Uranium-series dating of gypsum speleothems: methodology and examples

methods.description: This method describes the preparation of gypsum speleothem samples for uranium-series dating using ICP-MS techniques. The procedure involves the use of actinide-specific resins to simplify chemical workup and minimize contamination.

steps.description: Introduction of new, actinide-specific resins like the Eichrom TRU resin to simplify the chemical workup procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: use actinide-specific resins for chemical workup

steps.description: Actinides are selectively retained directly from 1M HNO3. Matrix ions (SO4 2- and Ca 2+) can then be washed out with 1M HNO3.
steps.searchable_summary: retain actinides and wash out matrix ions using 1M HNO3

steps.description: Convert the eluant from a nitrate to a chloride base and subsequently elute actinides by dilute HCl/HF.
steps.searchable_summary: convert eluant to chloride base and elute actinides

steps.description: Test the solubility of gypsum directly in 1M HNO3 and dissolve up to 10 g gypsum in 600 mL acid.
After spiking and equilibration, retain actinides on a small column.
steps.searchable_summary: retain actinides on column after spiking and equilibration

steps.description: Wash and elute the solution, then dry down with HNO3 and dissolve in 2% HNO3 for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: wash, elute, dry, and dissolve solution for analysis
